sources.full: Paradox Succubus, Master of the Open Palm

monster.name: Seraviel, the Paradox Succubus

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the Paradox Succubus fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Paradox Ascension
entries: The first time the Paradox Succubus is reduced to 0 Hit Points, she doesn't die: the mortal monk's discipline burns away and the paradox pours through her. At the start of her next turn she rises as {@creature Seraviel, Unbound|ParadoxSuccubus} in the same space, at that form's full Hit Points, with all conditions on her ended. Run both forms as one boss; award the encounter's XP only when the Unbound form falls.

trait.name: Two Natures (Monk and Fiend)
entries: The Paradox Succubus is a master of the Way of the Open Palm who is also a true fiend. Each turn she can fight as a {@b monk} (Multiattack and Flurry of Blows), as a {@b succubus/incubus} (Dominate, Draining Kiss, Nightmare, Etherealness, Shape-Shift), or blend the two. Run her however best presses the party that round.

trait.name: Channel Duality
entries: She channels holy ki through one open palm and her fiend nature through the other, so her unarmed strikes carry both Radiant and Necrotic energy (included below). Her Radiant and Necrotic damage ignores Resistance to those types.

trait.name: Diamond Soul
entries: The Paradox Succubus is proficient in all saving throws (already included).

trait.name: Evasion
entries: When she makes a Dexterity saving throw to take half damage, she instead takes none on a success and only half on a failure.

trait.name: Shape-Shift
entries: As a Bonus Action, she shape-shifts into a Small or Medium Humanoid she has seen, or back into her true fiend form. Her statistics are the same in each form except she loses her flying Speed while Humanoid. Anything she is wearing or carrying transforms with her. She reverts to her true form when she dies.

trait.name: Telepathic Bond
entries: While on the same plane of existence as a creature she has Charmed, Dominated, or kissed, the Paradox Succubus can communicate with it telepathically over any distance.

trait.name: Paradox Corruption (Curse)
entries: A creature cursed by her Annihilation Palm gains 1 Corruption level: a cumulative {@b −2} penalty to attack rolls, saving throws, and spell save DCs, and it can't regain Hit Points while cursed. At 5 levels it is recopied into a paradox-touched thrall (removed from play). {@spell greater restoration|XPHB} or level-5+ {@spell remove curse|XPHB} removes one level; {@spell wish|XPHB} removes all.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Paradox Succubus makes two attacks, in any combination of Open Palm Strike, Fiendish Touch, and Nightmare Touch. She can replace one of those attacks with Dominate or Draining Kiss, or with Annihilation Palm if it is available.

action.name: Open Palm Strike
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 1d12 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Radiant damage and 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Fiendish Touch
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) Psychic damage. (The succubus's signature touch.)

action.name: Nightmare Touch
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 13}, reach 5 ft. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d6 + 6}) Psychic damage, and if the target is {@condition charmed} by the Paradox Succubus it can't take Reactions until the end of its next turn as waking nightmares close in. (The incubus's signature touch.)

action.name: Dominate
entries: The Paradox Succubus casts {@spell dominate monster|XPHB} (no Material components), spell save {@dc 21}, on one creature she can see within 60 feet. (This is the succubus/incubus Charm, upgraded: she takes the mind, not just the heart. It requires her Concentration; damaging her or the target forces or allows the usual saves.)

action.name: Draining Kiss
entries: The Paradox Succubus kisses a creature {@condition charmed} or Dominated by her, or a creature {@condition grappled} by her, {@condition incapacitated}, or willing, within 5 feet. {@actSave con} {@dc 21}. {@actSaveFail} 33 ({@damage 6d10}) Psychic damage; {@actSaveSuccess} half as much. Either way the target's Hit Point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the damage taken (until it finishes a Short or Long Rest) and the Paradox Succubus regains that many Hit Points. The target dies if this reduces its Hit Point maximum to 0.

action.name: Annihilation Palm {@recharge 6}
entries: The Paradox Succubus brings both palms together on one creature within 5 feet, and holy and fiendish energy meet and annihilate. {@b This attack hits automatically} (but is never a Critical Hit, even against a {@condition prone} or {@condition unconscious} target), and on the turn she uses it she can't also use Flurry of Blows. The annihilation tears through Force/energy: any {@spell wall of force|XPHB}, {@spell forcecage|XPHB}, or similar Force effect between her and the target is destroyed, and the target gains no benefit from the {@spell shield|XPHB} spell or from cover made of Force against this attack. The target takes 91 ({@damage 26d6}) Force damage—the raw energy where her radiant and necrotic palms meet and annihilate, a blast to rival an ancient dragon's breath—then makes two saving throws:

items.name: Constitution {@dc 20} (the curse / death).
items.entry: On a failure, roll {@dice d100}: {@b 01–10}, the target dies (Death); {@b 11–35}, the target gains 1 level of Paradox Corruption (Curse; see trait); {@b 36–00}, no additional effect. On a success, the target takes only half the Force damage and isn't subject to the d100 roll.

items.name: Strength {@dc 20} (the impact).
items.entry: On a failure, the target is hurled 100 feet straight away from her and knocked {@condition prone}; if it strikes a solid surface or another creature first, it stops there and takes an extra 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Bludgeoning damage (and a creature it hits makes the same Strength save or is also knocked {@condition prone}).

action.name: Etherealness
entries: The Paradox Succubus enters the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane, or vice versa. She remains in the Border Ethereal, where she can see and move but can't affect or be affected by anything on the other plane.

bonus.name: Flurry of Blows
entries: The Paradox Succubus makes three Open Palm Strikes. Each creature hit by one of these strikes makes a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw or suffers one effect of her choice (per target): knocked {@condition prone}; pushed up to 15 feet; or unable to take {@b Reactions} until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Nightmare
entries: The Paradox Succubus invades the mind of one creature she can see within 60 feet. {@actSave wis} {@dc 21}. {@actSaveFail} 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Psychic damage; or, if the target is already {@condition charmed} or Dominated by her, it instead falls {@condition unconscious} for up to 1 minute (it repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, waking on a success, and also wakes if it takes damage or a creature uses an action to rouse it). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and no sleep. (The incubus's dream-haunting.)

bonus.name: Step of the Wind
entries: The Paradox Succubus takes the {@action Dash} and {@action Disengage} actions, and her jump distance is doubled for the turn.

reaction.name: Evasive Flow
entries: When a creature the Paradox Succubus can see makes an attack roll against her, she moves up to half her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. If this movement takes her out of the attack's reach or range, the attack misses (and is wasted); otherwise, the attacker has Disadvantage on that attack. Once per round.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the Paradox Succubus can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Flowing Step
entries: The Paradox Succubus moves up to her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

legendary.name: Palm
entries: The Paradox Succubus makes one Open Palm Strike, Fiendish Touch, or Nightmare Touch. She can use Palm no more than once per round.

legendary.name: Beguile (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Paradox Succubus uses Nightmare, or casts Dominate.

entries.name: The Open Palm of Heaven and Ruin
entries: Seraviel was a succubus—an incubus, a lover, a liar, whatever the dream required—until she walked into a paradox-corrupted monastery and stayed to learn. She mastered the Way of the Open Palm and learned to draw holy ki through her fiend's hands, and the paradox rewarded the contradiction: now one palm shines and the other rots, and when she claps them together the two energies meet and unmake whatever is between them. She still seduces, still haunts dreams, still drinks hearts dry—but mostly she fights, beautifully, like a saint and a devil sharing one body. Cut her down and the discipline burns away, and what's left is only the paradox, unbound.

entries.name: Design: Two-Phase Solo Boss for a Legendary-Equipped Level-17 Party
entries: Built as a hard two-phase solo for four 17th-level characters with legendary items. {@b Phase 1} (this block, CR 21) is the disciplined monk-fiend; at 0 HP she ascends (Paradox Ascension) to {@creature Seraviel, Unbound|ParadoxSuccubus} ({@b Phase 2}, CR 23) at full Hit Points. Run both as one boss; expect a Vorthrax-tier set-piece. 2024 DMG: 4x level 17 budgets are Low 18,000 / Moderate 28,800 / High 46,800 XP; the two phases together sit well past High, which is the point of a climactic two-life boss.

{@b She carries the full Succubus + Incubus kit} (2025 Monster Manual): Fiendish Touch (succubus) and Nightmare Touch (incubus) as fiendish strikes; Dominate (the 2025 Charm, upgraded to {@spell dominate monster|XPHB}); Draining Kiss (heals her, needs a charmed/dominated/grappled target); Nightmare (the incubus dream-haunt—psychic, or sleep if already charmed); plus Shape-Shift, Telepathic Bond, and Etherealness. On top of that she is an Open Hand monk: up to five 1d12+Radiant+Necrotic strikes a round (Multiattack + Flurry, with prone/push/no-reaction riders) and the auto-hit Annihilation Palm finisher (91 Force—ancient-dragon-breath magnitude—the Granveyra-parity d100 Death/Corruption curse, plus a Strength save vs a 100-ft launch; single-target, halved on the Con save).

{@b Built-in anti-TPK guards (after adversarial playtest tuning):} 2 Legendary Resistances per phase (not 3); legendary Palm fires at most once per round; she can't Flurry on the turn she uses Annihilation Palm; Phase 2 keeps the 5-strike cadence (2 Multiattack + 3 Flurry) and {@i resists} Radiant rather than being immune, so radiant builds still matter; Annihilation Palm is recharge 6 in both phases; and Nightmare's sleep lasts at most 1 minute with a save every turn (no free encounter-removal via Dominate + sleep). {@b DM dials.} Too hard for a standard party: treat Annihilation's 01–10 as Corruption instead of Death (the d100 Death is kept on by default as the requested Granveyra-parity finisher), or skip the Phase-2 ascension. Too easy: restore 3 Legendary Resistances and Phase 2's 6-strike Multiattack, or have her open with Dominate on the party's striker.

monster.name: Seraviel, Unbound

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If she fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead. (This form's uses are fresh.)

trait.name: The End (Final Form)
entries: This is the Paradox Succubus's last form. When the Unbound is reduced to 0 Hit Points, she is destroyed for good.

trait.name: Beguiling Aura
entries: Her presence is overwhelming. At the start of each of its turns, a creature within 10 feet of the Paradox Succubus makes a {@dc 22} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it is {@condition charmed} by her until the start of its next turn; while charmed this way it can't attack her or target her with harmful abilities or spells (and she becomes a valid target for her own Draining Kiss and Nightmare). A creature that succeeds is unaffected by this aura until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Unmade Energy
entries: In her Unbound form the holy and fiendish energies have fully merged: her Open Palm Strikes and Annihilation Palm deal Force damage, and that Force damage ignores Resistance to Force, Radiant, and Necrotic damage.

trait.name: Diamond Soul
entries: She is proficient in all saving throws (already included).

trait.name: Evasion
entries: When she makes a Dexterity saving throw to take half damage, she instead takes none on a success and only half on a failure.

trait.name: Telepathic Bond
entries: While on the same plane as a creature she has Charmed, Dominated, or kissed, she can communicate with it telepathically over any distance.

trait.name: Paradox Corruption (Curse)
entries: A creature cursed by her Annihilation Palm gains 1 Corruption level: a cumulative {@b −2} penalty to attack rolls, saving throws, and spell save DCs, and it can't regain Hit Points while cursed. At 5 levels it is recopied into a paradox-touched thrall (removed from play). {@spell greater restoration|XPHB} or level-5+ {@spell remove curse|XPHB} removes one level; {@spell wish|XPHB} removes all.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Unbound makes two attacks, in any combination of Open Palm Strike, Fiendish Touch, and Nightmare Touch. She can replace one of those attacks with Dominate or Draining Kiss, or with Annihilation Palm if it is available.

action.name: Open Palm Strike
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, reach 5 ft. {@h}23 ({@damage 3d10 + 7}) Force damage—the merged annihilation energy of her two palms.

action.name: Fiendish Touch
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, reach 5 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 2d10 + 7}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Nightmare Touch
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 14}, reach 5 ft. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d6 + 7}) Psychic damage, and a {@condition charmed} target can't take Reactions until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Dominate
entries: She casts {@spell dominate monster|XPHB} (no Material components), spell save {@dc 22}, on one creature she can see within 60 feet (requires Concentration).

action.name: Draining Kiss
entries: She kisses a creature {@condition charmed} or Dominated by her, or {@condition grappled} by her, {@condition incapacitated}, or willing, within 5 feet. {@actSave con} {@dc 22}. {@actSaveFail} 38 ({@damage 7d10}) Psychic damage; {@actSaveSuccess} half. Either way the target's Hit Point maximum drops by the damage taken (until it finishes a Short or Long Rest) and she regains that many Hit Points; the target dies if its Hit Point maximum drops to 0.

action.name: Annihilation Palm {@recharge 6}
entries: She brings both palms together on one creature within 5 feet. {@b This attack hits automatically} (but is never a Critical Hit, even against a {@condition prone} or {@condition unconscious} target), and on the turn she uses it she can't also use Flurry of Blows. The annihilation tears through Force/energy: any {@spell wall of force|XPHB}, {@spell forcecage|XPHB}, or similar Force effect between her and the target is destroyed, and the target gains no benefit from the {@spell shield|XPHB} spell or from cover made of Force against this attack. The target takes 98 ({@damage 28d6}) Force damage—holy and fiendish energy annihilating—then makes two saving throws:

items.name: Constitution {@dc 21} (the curse / death).
items.entry: On a failure, roll {@dice d100}: {@b 01–10}, the target dies (Death); {@b 11–35}, it gains 1 level of Paradox Corruption (Curse); {@b 36–00}, no additional effect. On a success, half Force damage and no d100.

items.name: Strength {@dc 21} (the impact).
items.entry: On a failure, the target is hurled 100 feet straight away and knocked {@condition prone}; if it strikes a surface or creature first it stops and takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Bludgeoning damage.

bonus.name: Flurry of Blows
entries: The Unbound makes three Open Palm Strikes. Each creature hit makes a {@dc 21} Dexterity saving throw or suffers one effect of her choice (per target): knocked {@condition prone}; pushed up to 15 feet; or unable to take {@b Reactions} until the end of its next turn.

bonus.name: Nightmare
entries: She invades the mind of one creature she can see within 60 feet. {@actSave wis} {@dc 22}. {@actSaveFail} 22 ({@damage 5d8}) Psychic damage; or, if the target is already {@condition charmed} or Dominated by her, it instead falls {@condition unconscious} for up to 1 minute (it repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, waking on a success, and also wakes if it takes damage). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage and no sleep.

bonus.name: Step of the Wind
entries: She takes the {@action Dash} and {@action Disengage} actions; her jump distance is doubled.

reaction.name: Evasive Flow
entries: When a creature she can see makes an attack roll against her, she moves up to half her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. If this takes her out of reach or range, the attack misses; otherwise the attacker has Disadvantage. Once per round.

legendaryHeader: Legendary Action Uses: 3. Immediately after another creature's turn, the Unbound can expend a use to take one of the following actions. She regains all expended uses at the start of each of her turns.

legendary.name: Flowing Step
entries: She moves up to her Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

legendary.name: Palm
entries: She makes one Open Palm Strike, Fiendish Touch, or Nightmare Touch. No more than once per round.

legendary.name: Beguile (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: She uses Nightmare, or casts Dominate.

entries.name: Unbound
entries: With the monk's restraint gone, there is nothing left holding the two energies apart—she is a blur of light and rot, faster, crueler, leaking annihilation into the air around her. The saint and the devil have stopped taking turns. Now they scream at once.
